summary: Iteration 16 — Backend-only verification of TWO bug fixes from the user's RUT-job RCA: (a) tracker OS case-fold at server.py:9855 (visitor_os 'Android' (title-case from detect_device) vs allowed_os ['android'] (lowercase as stored) was rejecting 100% of valid Android visits with a 'Device Restricted' page); (b) form_filler._page_has_captcha rewritten to use widget-aware regex CAPTCHA_PATTERNS at form_filler.py:64 — the pre-fix bare 'challenge-platform' substring match was producing 71/100 false 'skipped_captcha' tags because Cloudflare/Emergent's preview-pod edge injects '/cdn-cgi/challenge-platform/scripts/jsd/main.js' (passive bot analytics) into every response. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iteration16_os_fold_and_captcha.py — 23/23 pytest passed in 2.12s. Iter14+15 regression also re-run: 35/35 passed. Coverage: (1) Android UA + android-allowed link → no Device Restricted page (the actual bug repro & fix proof), (2) iOS UA + android-allowed link → still 403 + 'Device Restricted' + 'Your device: iOS' (not over-permissive), (3) Android UA + ios-allowed link → still 403 (mirror), (4) iOS UA + ios-allowed link → no block; CAPTCHA_PATTERNS true-negatives: bare jsd injection / prose with word 'captcha' / empty page / normal login form, all return False; CAPTCHA_PATTERNS true-positives: cf-turnstile div, g-recaptcha div, h-captcha div, challenges.cloudflare.com iframe src, google.com/recaptcha iframe src, hcaptcha.com iframe src, __cf_chl_jschl_tk__, __cf_chl_managed_tk__, cf-mitigated meta, iframe title='reCAPTCHA' — all return True. Regression: admin login, GET engine-status (still returns ready, no browser_path leak), POST engine-prewarm (still already_ready idempotent), GET RUT jobs (no 5xx), unknown short_code (still 404). Test isolation: each tracker test uses a fresh random public IPv4 in X-Forwarded-For (203.0.x.y) to avoid the cross-DB duplicate-IP-blocker — verified working. Seed: two TEST_iter16_* tracking links inserted directly into db.links (admin token cannot POST /api/links, that endpoint is user-scoped) and torn down in module-fixture finally.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:9864-9865 — case-fold fix is correct: `[str(s).strip().lower() for s in allowed_os]` and `(visitor_os or '').strip().lower()`. Defensive .strip() on both sides handles whitespace in stored config values too. The display string `allowed_display` still uses the raw allowed_os list (not the normalised one) — good UX, the user sees their original casing on the block page.

server.py — note: allowed_os values are normalized to lowercase ALREADY at LINK CREATION (line 3550: `allowed_os_list = [o.strip().lower() for o in (allowed_os or '').split(',') if o.strip()]`) for the form-data /links endpoint. So the OS-fold fix at line 9864 is mainly belt-and-suspenders for older links that may have been seeded/migrated with mixed case. Both layers cooperate correctly. LGTM.

form_filler.py:64-80 — CAPTCHA_PATTERNS rewrite is solid. Captures all four common widget surfaces (Turnstile, reCAPTCHA, hCaptcha, CF interstitial) and explicitly excludes the passive jsd injection. Regex uses re.I and word-boundaries (\b) on class names so 'g-recaptcha-fake' would also match — but that's actually fine because such a class name would still likely indicate intent. The patterns matching iframe src URLs require the canonical hostnames so a proxied/mirrored copy on a different host would slip through, but that's an extreme edge case and out of scope.

form_filler.py:74-79 — `cf-mitigated|cf-error-details` is a broad pattern. It matches the literal substring anywhere — including inside <meta name="cf-mitigated"> headers AND inside any `<style>.cf-mitigated{...}</style>` rule. Acceptable trade-off (no observed false positive in real CF responses) but worth noting if a future false positive appears in the wild.

ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking) — Consider a unit test inside form_filler.py module itself (e.g. `if __name__ == '__main__':` self-check) so the regex set never silently regresses if someone edits it. The new iter16 test file already covers it externally so this is purely a developer-ergonomics nit.

ENHANCEMENT (non-blocking) — server.py:9855 OS check could ALSO normalise on the boolean side (e.g. `os_alias = {'android':{'android'}, 'ios':{'ios','iphoneos','ipados'}}`) to be future-proof for iPadOS UA strings (user-agents lib typically returns 'iOS' for iPad too — current behaviour OK, just flagging).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration16_os_fold_and_captcha.py (new — 23 tests across 4 classes: OS-fold tracker (4 tests covering android/ios × allowed/blocked combos), captcha true-negatives (4 tests including the EXACT preview-pod jsd injection that caused the user's bug), captcha true-positives (10 tests covering all major real challenge surfaces), prior-iteration regression (5 tests: admin login, engine-status, engine-prewarm, RUT jobs list, 404 on unknown short_code))
test_credentials: Admin: admin@trackmaster.local / admin123 via POST /api/admin/login (read from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: Two tracking links seeded DIRECTLY in MongoDB db.links (TEST_iter16_and_<hex8>, TEST_iter16_ios_<hex8>) under a synthetic user_id TEST_iter16_<hex8>. Cleaned up in module fixture teardown via delete_many. No links created via /api/links endpoint because that requires a regular user with the 'links' feature flag, while the admin /api/admin/login token is admin-only. Each tracker request uses a fresh random 203.0.x.y X-Forwarded-For IP to bypass the cross-DB duplicate-IP blocker.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run: cd /app/backend && python -m pytest tests/test_iteration16_os_fold_and_captcha.py tests/test_iteration14_rut_engine_status.py tests/test_iteration15_rut_engine_prewarm.py -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/iteration16.xml. Requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL (frontend/.env), MONGO_URL+DB_NAME (backend/.env). The OS-fold tests assert on response BODY (`'Device Restricted' not in r.text`) rather than status code alone because a successful tracker visit returns an HTML interstitial that runs the offer redirect via JS — status can be 200/302 but the success signal is the absence of the 'Device Restricted' heading. CAPTCHA_PATTERNS tests load form_filler via importlib so they don't import the heavy playwright runtime — fast (<1s). If link 2735ad44 is ever re-seeded by the user, the iter16 tests still pass because they create their own isolated TEST_iter16_* links.
rca of the issue: User uploaded RUT zip showing 71/100 'skipped_captcha' + 0 conversions on link 2735ad44 (allowed_os=['android'], proxies+UAs all Android). RCA: TWO independent bugs compounded — (1) tracker OS check at server.py:9855 used `visitor_os not in allowed_os` where visitor_os='Android' (detect_device returns title-case from ua_parser at server.py:1864) but allowed_os=['android'] (lowercase from form normalisation at server.py:3550) → 100% of Android visits were sent to the Device Restricted 403 HTML, never reaching the offer URL, never logging a conversion; (2) form_filler._page_has_captcha matched the bare substring 'challenge-platform' which Cloudflare/Emergent's preview-pod edge injects via /cdn-cgi/challenge-platform/scripts/jsd/main.js (passive bot analytics, harmless) into EVERY response → caused 71/100 false 'skipped_captcha' tags even on non-blocked visits. Mitigation applied by main agent and verified in this iteration: (a) lowercased both sides of the OS comparison at server.py:9864-9865 with defensive .strip(); (b) replaced substring match with widget-aware CAPTCHA_PATTERNS regex list (form_filler.py:64-80) targeting only iframes on canonical challenge hosts, .cf-turnstile/.g-recaptcha/.h-captcha class widgets, and real CF interstitial markers (__cf_chl_jschl_tk__, cf-mitigated, etc.). Reproduction in test: TestOsFoldTracker::test_android_ua_on_android_allowed_link_is_NOT_device_restricted directly proves the bug fix; TestCaptchaDetectorTrueNegatives::test_cloudflare_passive_jsd_injection_is_not_captcha uses the exact preview-pod injection string and asserts False.
